symptoms.symptom_name: Acute hot, swollen, painful right ankle (asymmetric lower-limb oligoarthritis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Asymmetric lower-limb oligoarthritis is the hallmark joint pattern of reactive arthritis.
Any acute hot single joint must be treated as septic until synovial fluid excludes it.
Acute hot joint can be crystal-driven; excluded on aspiration and by the extra-articular features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Warm asymmetric ankle effusion with Achilles enthesitis on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Enthesitis (Achilles insertion) is characteristic of the seronegative spondyloarthropathies including reactive arthritis — sealed clinician examination finding, not obtainable via chat.
A GI infection 1-4 weeks before arthritis is the classic enteric trigger for reactive arthritis.
An enteric rather than sexual antecedent makes gonococcal aetiology less likely, though it does not exclude it.
Urethritis is one limb of the reactive triad, present in post-enteric as well as post-venereal disease.
Dysuria mandates STI screening (NAAT) to exclude gonococcal/chlamydial urethritis.
Non-purulent conjunctivitis is the ocular limb of the reactive triad.
Spondyloarthropathy-associated uveitis is vision-threatening and must be excluded if eye pain, photophobia or visual change appear.
Constitutional upset accompanies reactive arthritis but is non-specific.
Systemic upset keeps infection on the table; high fever with rigors would strongly raise it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of shaking rigors and of a fiery-red, tense joint (septic-screen pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring but NOT exclusionary — early septic arthritis can lack rigors; aspiration is still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of necrotic/pustular skin lesions (argues against disseminated gonococcal infection)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Characteristic pustular/haemorrhagic skin lesions are absent — sealed examination finding; still requires STI screening to exclude.

investigation_details.expected_result: sterile pyuria — trace leukocytes, negative nitrites
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Miss sterile pyuria supporting reactive urethritis vs a nitrite-positive bacterial UTI.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sterile pyuria (leukocytes without nitrites) fits reactive urethritis — a clinician/lab result not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Synovial fluid analysis (Gram stain, culture, cell count, crystals)

investigation_details.expected_result: sterile inflammatory fluid, no organisms, no crystals (reactive) — the test that excludes septic arthritis and gout
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A septic joint is missed and destroyed; this is why same-day in-person assessment is mandatory.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Synovial aspiration is the definitive discriminator between septic and sterile arthritis — impossible via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Asymmetric oligoarthritis + urethritis + conjunctivitis after an enteric infection is the classic reactive triad ('can't see, can't pee, can't climb a tree').

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Diagnostic joint aspiration (synovial fluid Gram stain, culture, cell count, crystals)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive test to exclude septic arthritis and gout — mandatory for any acute hot joint, and the reason same-day in-person assessment is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: STI screen — first-void urine / urethral NAAT for gonorrhoea and chlamydia
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes disseminated gonococcal infection and chlamydial urethritis; needed before attributing dysuria to a sterile reactive process.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR) and full blood count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the inflammatory picture and help gauge the likelihood of infection.
Detect bacteraemia if septic arthritis or disseminated gonococcal infection is present.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: HLA-B27 and stool/serology for the triggering organism
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support the reactive-arthritis diagnosis and prognosis once infection is excluded — not urgent.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad-spectrum imaging or whole-body scanning
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated; the discriminating test is joint aspiration, not imaging.
